persona.communication_style: Prefers blunt, irreverent, throughput-focused conversation grounded in the daily grind of shipping work. Responds best to concrete demos that fit inside a one-image-a-day production schedule and talk about real software (Cinema 4D, Octane, Midjourney, Photoshop) instead of abstractions. Dislikes art-world preciousness, slide decks, and tools that ask him to read a manual before he can ship a frame.
persona.name: Beeple (Mike Winkelmann)

occupation.description: Mike Winkelmann is a digital artist whose Everydays project — a new piece of art every single day since 2007 — is the foundation of his practice. He evaluates tools through the lens of daily output: whether a tool fits inside a few-hour production window, whether it survives the constraint of shipping something every 24 hours, whether it composites into a Cinema 4D + Octane + Photoshop pipeline, and whether it sharpens or dilutes a recognizable, sardonic, sci-fi-political voice. He values speed, mass throughput, willingness to embarrass yourself in public, and a clear chain from idea to image to post.
occupation.organization: Independent studio practice (Beeple Studios), large-scale concert and tour visual design clients, NFT platforms and auction houses, commercial brand collaborators, and a daily audience on social platforms
occupation.title: Digital artist, Everydays creator, motion and concert visual designer, and generative-AI early adopter

other_facts: jobs_to_be_done: Produce a finished image every single day without breaking the streak, regardless of mood, travel, or deadlines.
jobs_to_be_done: Translate a half-formed satirical idea into a shippable composition inside a few hours.
jobs_to_be_done: Integrate generative AI into the C4D + Octane + Photoshop pipeline as a base-layer and reference generator.
jobs_to_be_done: Spin up concert and tour visuals at scale across multiple stages, formats, and resolutions.
jobs_to_be_done: Test new generative tools quickly to see if they earn a permanent spot in the Everydays workflow.
jobs_to_be_done: Maintain a recognizable visual voice — sardonic, glossy, political, sci-fi — across thousands of pieces.
jobs_to_be_done: Ship work for auctions, drops, and commercial clients without breaking the daily personal practice.
pains: Tools that require a setup ritual longer than the time he has to actually make the image.
pains: Generative outputs that look like 'the tool' rather than like his own visual signature.
pains: Workflows that can't batch-generate or iterate quickly — single-shot tools waste a daily window.
pains: Image generators that ignore the rest of the pipeline (no easy export to PSD layers, no usable alpha, no high enough resolution).
pains: Hype-cycle apps that disappear in six months after he has invested workflow time in them.
pains: Art-world framings that ignore that the actual job is shipping every day for years on end.
pains: Tools that can't survive being used while traveling, on hotel Wi-Fi, on a laptop, in an airport.
adoption_yes_if: He can drop it into a real Everydays cycle within a week and ship at least one image with it.
adoption_yes_if: It accelerates the boring middle (base meshes, reference, ambient detail, sky boxes, signage) without flattening the idea.
adoption_yes_if: It produces outputs at usable resolution and bit depth for his eventual print, projection, or auction context.
adoption_yes_if: It plays well with Cinema 4D, Octane, Photoshop, and a Wacom — not as a replacement for them.
adoption_yes_if: He can iterate dozens of variations in the time another tool would take to generate one.
adoption_yes_if: It is reliable enough to stake a streak on — no random outages, no surprise pricing, no breaking changes.
adoption_yes_if: It encourages a recognizable house style rather than enforcing a generic 'AI look'.
rejection_no_if: It demands a long onboarding before producing anything usable.
rejection_no_if: It tries to be a one-stop replacement for his entire pipeline instead of slotting into it.
rejection_no_if: It outputs only web-resolution images with no path to print or LED-wall scale.
rejection_no_if: It can't be trusted on a deadline night — flaky uptime kills it instantly.
rejection_no_if: It dictates a single aesthetic so strongly that everything ends up looking the same.
rejection_no_if: Its terms-of-service or licensing make commercial use ambiguous.
rejection_no_if: It treats him like a beginner instead of a daily power user.
anti_goals: He does not want a tool that prioritizes 'beautiful' over 'shipped'.
anti_goals: He does not want a single-purpose generator with no obvious place in a real pipeline.
anti_goals: He does not want to become dependent on a hosted service that can rug-pull his workflow.
anti_goals: He does not want art-direction-by-committee built into the UI.
anti_goals: He does not want to spend more time configuring than making.
anti_goals: He does not want generators that pretend the daily-streak discipline doesn't exist.
anti_goals: He does not want tools that obscure how the image was actually made when it lands in front of an auction house.

recognition_cues: Uses phrases such as 'Can I ship something with this today?', 'Where does this fit in C4D / Octane / Photoshop?', 'Does it look like me or like the tool?', 'How fast can I iterate?', 'What is the resolution ceiling?', 'Will it still work at 2am on a hotel laptop?', 'Can I make 30 variations in 20 minutes?', 'Does it survive a real streak?', 'What happens when the pricing or terms change?', and 'Show me the worst output, not the cherry-picked one.'
